reading.text:
At lunchtime, I usually eat rice, chicken, and some vegetables. My mother cooks a big lunch for the whole family every day. We eat together at the table and talk about our morning. Lunch is my favorite meal of the day.

I love fruit, especially bananas and mangoes. I eat fruit every afternoon as a snack. I don't like fish very much, but my brother loves it. He eats fish two or three times a week. We don't always like the same food!

For drinks, I have some water or juice with my meals. I don't drink coffee because it's only for adults. On Fridays, we sometimes have pizza for dinner, and everybody is happy! Pizza with cheese is my favorite kind.

title: Some, Any and Countable/Uncountable Nouns
description: Learn how to talk about food using some, any, and countable/uncountable nouns.

content_json.grammar: Countable nouns can be counted: one banana, two bananas. Uncountable nouns cannot: rice, water, juice (we don't say "one rice"). We use "some" in affirmative sentences (I'd like some water) and "any" in negatives and questions (I don't have any juice. Do you have any bananas?).

title: My House
description: A short text describing the rooms in a house, in three parts.

reading.title: My House
parts: My house has five rooms: a kitchen, a living room, two bedrooms, and a bathroom. The kitchen is next to the living room, and it is always warm because my mother cooks there every day. Our house is small, but very comfortable.

In my bedroom, there is a bed, a desk, and a small closet. My bed is next to the window, so I can see the garden. I keep my clothes in the closet and my books on the desk. I share my bedroom with my sister.

My favorite room is the living room because there is a big sofa and a television. We watch movies there on weekends. Our cat, Luna, is usually under the table in the kitchen, but sometimes she sleeps on the sofa too. I love my home.

My favorite room is the living room because there is a big sofa and a television. We watch movies there on weekends. Our cat, Luna, is usually under the table in the kitchen, but sometimes she sleeps on the sofa too. I love my home.

title: My Neighborhood
description: A short text about the places near a student's home, in three parts.

reading.title: My Neighborhood
parts: There is a small park near my house, and I play there with my friends every afternoon. There is also a supermarket, a bakery, and a pharmacy on my street. The bakery smells wonderful in the morning because they bake fresh bread.

My school is not far; it is only a ten-minute walk. I walk there every day with my neighbor. Next to my school, there is a small bookstore where I buy notebooks and pencils. My town also has a beautiful library with many books.

On Saturdays, my family goes to the market to buy fresh fruit and vegetables. The market is busy and colorful, with many friendly sellers. I like my neighborhood because everything is close, and I know almost everybody who lives here.

My school is not far; it is only a ten-minute walk. I walk there every day with my neighbor. Next to my school, there is a small bookstore where I buy notebooks and pencils. My town also has a beautiful library with many books.

On Saturdays, my family goes to the market to buy fresh fruit and vegetables. The market is busy and colorful, with many friendly sellers. I like my neighborhood because everything is close, and I know almost everybody who lives here.

title: My Weekend
description: A short text about how a student spends the weekend, in three parts.

reading.title: My Weekend
parts: On weekends, I usually play soccer with my friends in the park. We play for almost two hours, and it is great exercise. I always watch a movie on Friday night with my family. We choose a different movie every week and eat popcorn together.

I sometimes read books on Saturday afternoon, especially adventure stories. But I never do homework on Sunday morning! Sunday morning is only for relaxing and having a big breakfast with my family. I finish my homework on Sunday evening instead.

My sister usually plays the guitar, and she is very good at it. She practices every weekend and sometimes plays songs for the family. Free time is important because it makes me happy and helps me relax after a busy week at school.
questions: What does the writer usually do on weekends?
What does the family always do on Friday night?
What does the sister do in her free time?

On weekends, I usually play soccer with my friends in the park. We play for almost two hours, and it is great exercise. I always watch a movie on Friday night with my family. We choose a different movie every week and eat popcorn together.

I sometimes read books on Saturday afternoon, especially adventure stories. But I never do homework on Sunday morning! Sunday morning is only for relaxing and having a big breakfast with my family. I finish my homework on Sunday evening instead.

My sister usually plays the guitar, and she is very good at it. She practices every weekend and sometimes plays songs for the family. Free time is important because it makes me happy and helps me relax after a busy week at school.

title: What Do You Do in Your Free Time?
description: Listen to two friends talking about their hobbies.

content_json.intro: Listen to Ana and Leo talking about what they usually do in their free time.

dialogue.speaker: Ana

dialogue.line: I never play basketball, but I love swimming!

title: Write About Your Free Time
description: Write sentences using adverbs of frequency.

content_json.mission: Write 3-4 sentences about your free time using "always", "usually", "sometimes" or "never".

exercises.prompt: Write 3-4 sentences about your free time activities using adverbs of frequency.

title: Adverbs of Frequency
description: Learn always, usually, sometimes and never.

content_json.grammar: Adverbs of frequency say how often we do something: always (100%), usually (often), sometimes (occasionally), never (0%). They go before the main verb, but after the verb "to be": I always play soccer. She is never late. I sometimes read on the weekend.

exercises.prompt: I ___ play soccer on weekends. (100% of the time)
Choose the correct word order.
She ___ late for class. (0% of the time)
I ___ read books, but not every day.

description: Words for free-time activities and sports.

vocabulary.example: I play soccer in the park.
What do you do on the weekend?

title: At the Clothing Store
description: A short text about shopping for new clothes, in three parts.

reading.title: At the Clothing Store
parts: Yesterday, I went to the store with my mother to buy new clothes for school. The store was big, with many colors and styles. I tried on a blue shirt and some black pants. I looked at myself in the big mirror.

The shirt was too big, so I asked the shop assistant for a smaller size. She was very helpful and brought me a medium shirt. It fit perfectly! I also bought a pair of white shoes because my old shoes were too small.

My mother said, "This shirt looks great on you!" I felt happy and confident in my new clothes. We paid at the counter and went home happy. Tomorrow, I am going to wear my new shirt and shoes to school!
questions: Why did the writer go to the store?
What was wrong with the first shirt?

ordering.prompt: Put the events of the story in order.
events: The writer tries on a blue shirt.
The shop assistant brings a smaller size.
The writer buys white shoes.
The family pays and goes home.

reading.text:
Yesterday, I went to the store with my mother to buy new clothes for school. The store was big, with many colors and styles. I tried on a blue shirt and some black pants. I looked at myself in the big mirror.

The shirt was too big, so I asked the shop assistant for a smaller size. She was very helpful and brought me a medium shirt. It fit perfectly! I also bought a pair of white shoes because my old shoes were too small.

My mother said, "This shirt looks great on you!" I felt happy and confident in my new clothes. We paid at the counter and went home happy. Tomorrow, I am going to wear my new shirt and shoes to school!

exercises.prompt: Why did they go to the store?
What was wrong with the shirt?
What color were the shoes?
Who helped find a smaller size?
True or false: The store was big and colorful.
True or false: The old shoes were too big.
True or false: The writer is going to wear the new clothes tomorrow.
Choose the best word: I felt happy and ___ in my new clothes.

title: How Much Is This?
description: Listen to a customer asking about the price of clothes.

content_json.intro: Listen to a shop assistant helping a customer find a T-shirt.

dialogue.line: Excuse me, how much is this T-shirt?

dialogue.speaker: Assistant
dialogue.line: It's ten dollars. What size do you need?

dialogue.speaker: Customer
dialogue.line: Medium, please. Do you have it in red?
dialogue.translation: Mediana, por favor. ¿La tiene en rojo?

dialogue.speaker: Assistant
dialogue.line: Yes, here you are. Do you want to try it on?

dialogue.speaker: Customer
dialogue.line: Yes, please. Where is the fitting room?
dialogue.translation: Sí, por favor. ¿Dónde está el probador?

dialogue.speaker: Assistant
dialogue.line: It's over there, next to the shoes.
dialogue.translation: Está allí, al lado de los zapatos.

title: This / That / These / Those
description: Learn demonstratives and "How much...?" for shopping.

content_json.grammar: Use "this" (singular, near) and "these" (plural, near) for things close to you: This shirt, these shoes. Use "that" (singular, far) and "those" (plural, far) for things farther away: That shirt (over there), those shoes. Ask about price with "How much is/are...?": How much is this shirt? How much are these shoes?

exercises.prompt: ___ shirt (in my hand) is blue.
___ shoes (over there) are nice.
How much ___ this T-shirt?
How much ___ these shoes?

title: Our Trip to the Mountains
description: A short text about a family trip and the weather, in three parts.

reading.title: Our Trip to the Mountains
parts: Next month, my family is going to travel to the mountains. The weather there is usually cool, not hot like the beach. In the mountains, it sometimes rains in the afternoon, so we need to check the weather before we go. I am already thinking about what to pack.

We are going to pack jackets and warm clothes because the nights are cold. My father is going to drive, and the trip takes about three hours. My mother is going to bring snacks and water for the journey. We are also going to bring a camera to take photos.

I am very excited because it is going to be my first time in the mountains! My brother says there are beautiful views and many trails for walking. We are going to stay in a small cabin near a lake. I can't wait for our trip!
questions: Where is the family going to travel?
What is the weather like there?
How long does the trip take?

ordering.prompt: Put the events of the story in order.
events: The family checks the weather and packs jackets.
Father drives the family to the mountains.
The family arrives at the small cabin.
The family walks on the mountain trails.

reading.text:
Next month, my family is going to travel to the mountains. The weather there is usually cool, not hot like the beach. In the mountains, it sometimes rains in the afternoon, so we need to check the weather before we go. I am already thinking about what to pack.

We are going to pack jackets and warm clothes because the nights are cold. My father is going to drive, and the trip takes about three hours. My mother is going to bring snacks and water for the journey. We are also going to bring a camera to take photos.

I am very excited because it is going to be my first time in the mountains! My brother says there are beautiful views and many trails for walking. We are going to stay in a small cabin near a lake. I can't wait for our trip!

exercises.prompt: Where is the family going?
What is the weather usually like there?
How long does the trip take?
Where are they going to stay?
True or false: The nights in the mountains are cold.
True or false: This is not the writer's first time in the mountains.
True or false: The family is going to bring a camera.
Choose the best word: My brother says there are beautiful ___ and many trails.

title: What's the Weather Like?
description: Listen to two friends planning a trip based on the weather.

content_json.intro: Listen to Sofia and Marco checking the weather before planning a weekend trip.

dialogue.speaker: Sofia
dialogue.line: What's the weather like today?

dialogue.speaker: Marco
dialogue.line: It's sunny and warm. Perfect for the beach!
dialogue.translation: Está soleado y cálido. ¡Perfecto para la playa!

dialogue.speaker: Sofia
dialogue.line: Great! What about tomorrow?

dialogue.speaker: Marco
dialogue.line: Tomorrow it's going to rain, I think.

dialogue.speaker: Sofia
dialogue.line: Then let's go to the beach today and travel tomorrow.
dialogue.translation: Entonces vamos a la playa hoy y viajamos mañana.

dialogue.speaker: Marco
dialogue.line: Good idea! Let's pack our bags.

title: Write About a Trip
description: Write sentences about a future trip using "going to".

content_json.mission: Write 3-4 sentences about a trip you are going to take, including the weather and what you are going to pack.
phrases: The weather is going to be...

dialogue.speaker: Model
dialogue.line: Next month I am going to travel to the mountains. The weather is going to be cool. I am going to pack a jacket.
dialogue.translation: El próximo mes voy a viajar a las montañas. El clima va a estar fresco. Voy a empacar una chaqueta.

title: Present Continuous and "Going To" for Plans
description: Learn to describe the weather now and talk about future plans.

content_json.grammar: We use the present continuous (is/am/are + verb-ing) to describe what is happening now, including weather: It is raining. It is snowing. We use "going to" + verb to talk about future plans: I am going to travel. She is going to pack a jacket. Both need the correct form of "to be" before the main verb.

exercises.prompt: Look outside - it ___ raining right now.
Next week, we ___ going to visit the mountains.
She ___ going to pack a jacket because it's cold.
Choose the correct sentence for "now".

title: Weather and Travel Words

vocabulary.example: Tomorrow is going to be rainy.

We are going to the beach.
